title: The Complaint of God
summary: The repository XML copies U-M CME item CME00089, which encodes only the 544-line poem on pp. 508-525 of Nils Edvard Borgstrom's 1911 Anglia article. Borgstrom directly transcribed Gonville and Caius College MS 174/95 and restored manuscript pp. 481-482's accidentally displaced lines to their signaled position after present line 320. DIMEV v1.0 records thirteen witnesses, but none besides Caius is made an unproved source of Borgstrom. Complete public facsimiles for five collateral witnesses are cached and mapped inside their whole codices: Trinity R.3.21, NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1, Lambeth MS 306, Harvard Houghton MS Eng 530, and Huntington HM 144. Furnivall's complete 1866 volume provides a separately modeled parallel edition of the two Lambeth witnesses.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00089
entities.description: Pinned repository artifact representing Borgstrom's poem selection.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00089
entities.description: Public electronic transcription of Borgstrom's poem pages, excluding the article's pp. 498-507 title and study.

entities.label: Nils Edvard Borgstrom, 'The Complaint of God to Sinful Man and the Answer of Man' (1911)
entities.description: Immediate source article, pp. 498-525; the poem is pp. 508-525. Borgstrom describes Caius 174/95, reconstructs its displaced passage, argues for Lichfield, analyzes language and meter, and prints a close but punctuated edition.

bibliographic.citation: Nils Edvard Borgstrom, 'The Complaint of God to Sinful Man and the Answer of Man. By William Lichfield,' Anglia 34 (1911): 498-525.
bibliographic.title: The Complaint of God to Sinful Man and the Answer of Man. By William Lichfield

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Anglia volume 34 (1911)
entities.description: Complete 544-page volume scan retaining the entire Borgstrom article and adjacent matter.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF with OCR and metadata derivatives

entities.label: Cambridge, Gonville and Caius College, MS 174/95
entities.description: Borgstrom's sole direct manuscript source. The poem is physically copied on manuscript pp. 469-480, but the scribe accidentally omitted present lines 321-364 and supplied them later on pp. 481-482 with cross-references to the intended insertion point. Borgstrom's continuous edition follows the corrected intellectual order rather than the codex sequence.

date_statements.label: Borgstrom's palaeographic description
date_statements.value: fifteenth-century hand, probably not the author's

physical_description.extent: Poem on manuscript pp. 469-482; DIMEV main locus pp. 469-480 with the displaced supply on pp. 481-482

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 record 4312 (IMEV 2711.5; NIMEV 2714)
entities.description: Versioned scholarly census describing the work as William Lychefeld's Complaint of God, sixty-eight abababab eight-line stanzas, and listing thirteen manuscript or early-print witnesses.

entities.label: Oxford, Corpus Christi College, MS 237
entities.description: Collateral Complaint witness at ff. 137v-146; not evidenced as a source used by Borgstrom.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Mid-fifteenth-century paper miscellany preserving an imperfect Complaint at ff. 3ra-6ra; lines 1-158 are lost with missing fols. 1-2.

physical_description.extent: iii + 247 + i leaves in its present state

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 facsimile (Scolar Press, 1979)
entities.description: Printed whole-manuscript facsimile with an introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson; no complete public digital copy was verified.

bibliographic.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (London: Scolar Press, 1979).
bibliographic.title: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38

entities.label: Magdalene College Cambridge, Pepys MS 1584
entities.description: Collateral witness at ff. 1v-13v, about sixty-eight stanzas with some six- and four-line groupings; the codex table and rubrics call it The Complaint of God.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.21
entities.description: Collateral seventy-two-stanza Complaint witness at ff. 182r-189r, with layered Lydgate and Lichfield attribution annotations. Direct facsimile inspection confirms the opening on f. 182r and explicit on f. 189r; f. 189v begins a different passion poem.

entities.label: Complete Trinity R.3.21 IIIF provider object
entities.description: Complete 686-canvas color sequence from front cover through envelope verso; the Complaint occupies canvases 369-383.
entities.reproduction_format: 686-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached at 1600 pixels

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2339
entities.description: First-half-fifteenth-century collateral witness at ff. 82r-100v, following a rubric naming the complaint of God to every sinful man and woman.

date_statements.label: current BL catalog
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: British Library Add MS 36983
entities.description: Damaged collateral witness at ff. 275rb-279va within a 305-folio Middle English miscellany.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: Circa-1480 paper manuscript copied and signed by Richard Heeg. Its imperfect Complaint begins at f. 158r with stanzas 1-6 wanting and continues through f. 170v, where DIMEV says the Song of Love to Jesus follows as if the same work.

physical_description.extent: xii + 216 + xviii folios; Complaint at ff. 158r-170v

entities.label: NLS complete digital facsimile of Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: Complete 482-page color PDF and matching 482-canvas IIIF sequence. The Complaint maps to PDF/IIIF pages 331-356, corresponding to ff. 158r-170v.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete color PDF and IIIF Presentation manifest

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 306
entities.description: Composite manuscript containing an incomplete Complaint at ff. 147r-152r. Direct inspection confirms the opening below a preceding explicit on f. 147r and the last surviving line on f. 152r; f. 152v begins The Stations of Rome.

entities.label: Lambeth complete public digital sequence for MS 306
entities.description: All 415 published color canvases, including covers, pastedowns, preliminary leaf, ff. 1r-204v, rear matter, and spine; the Complaint is canvases 297-307.
entities.reproduction_format: 415-canvas IIIF Presentation object cached mostly at 1800 pixels

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853
entities.description: Complete collateral Complaint at manuscript pp. 193-225; Furnivall prints this good text opposite the incomplete MS 306 version.

entities.label: Harvard Houghton Library MS Eng 530
entities.description: Circa-1450 paper miscellany whose Complaint begins imperfectly at f. 1ra and ends on f. 4r. Direct inspection maps the surviving text to canvases 9-15; another complaint begins on f. 4v / canvas 16.

entities.label: Harvard complete IIIF facsimile of Houghton MS Eng 530
entities.description: Complete current 432-canvas provider object from front board through rear board. The incomplete Complaint is canvases 9-15.
entities.reproduction_format: 432-canvas IIIF bundle at up to 1800 pixels

entities.label: Huntington Library MS HM 144
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-/early-sixteenth-century English paper miscellany containing a seventy-two-stanza Complaint at ff. 1r-9v. The item record and images preserve the opening rubric, explicit, and later Lichfield attribution.

entities.label: Huntington complete IIIF facsimile of HM 144
entities.description: Complete 319-canvas provider object including front cover, all published leaves and binding surfaces, and spine; the Complaint is canvases 12-29.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library Sel.5.6, Wynkyn de Worde's The remors of conscyence
entities.description: Imperfect circa-1510 early printed witness at fols. 2r-6v, substantially reworked and formerly indexed separately. It is collateral transmission, not Borgstrom's base.

bibliographic.citation: William Lichfield, The remors of conscyence (London: Wynkyn de Worde, circa 1510), STC 20881.3; imperfect Cambridge University Library copy Sel.5.6.
bibliographic.title: The remors of conscyence

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall, Political, Religious, and Love Poems (1866)
entities.description: Supporting parallel edition of the incomplete Lambeth MS 306 text and the fuller MS 853 text. Furnivall deliberately leaves the poorly arranged MS 306 version on even pages and prints MS 853 opposite it; this edition is collateral and not upstream of Borgstrom.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Political, Religious, and Love Poems, EETS o.s. 15 (London: N. Trubner for the Early English Text Society, 1866).
bibliographic.title: Political, Religious, and Love Poems
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trubner for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall's 1866 volume
entities.description: Complete 338-page image PDF containing the parallel Lambeth Complaint texts and full prefatory explanation.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Complaint of God text
rights.basis: The work and all recorded manuscript and early-print witnesses predate modern copyright by centuries.
notes: This does not settle rights or contractual terms in modern photographs, supplied reproductions, digital encodings, catalog data, or interface assets.

U-M digital text and item presentation

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and item presentation

The versioned release contains a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 license.
No item-specific delivery terms have been obtained; the College copyright policy normally limits supplied material to private research and requires written application for publication.
Published in 1979 without a verified open license; the medieval source is a separate layer.
CUL's supply terms limit normal orders to research/private use and require written permission for reproduction, including online publication.
The Pepys Digital Library asserts copyright in its images and requires purchase and permission for publication; no item-specific MS 1584 delivery terms were obtained.

rights.component: Trinity R.3.21 IIIF images and manifest
rights.basis: The exact IIIF object states CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution to Trinity College Cambridge.
notes: Commercial reuse or uses outside the license require separate authorization; Trinity's broader publication-permission wording is preserved as a provider-policy ambiguity.

rights.basis: The current catalog and digitisation route do not supply an item-specific open license for future reproductions.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript content represented by the NLS object
rights.basis: The exact NLS digital object applies the Public Domain Mark to the manuscript work.

rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to exact-page exceptions
rights.basis: The object page says images and transcriptions, including medium downloads, may be used under CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise stated.

rights.component: Lambeth MS 306 digital photographs and IIIF presentation

notes: Public viewing and private research caching do not authorize committing or redistributing the photographs.

rights.component: Harvard digital reproductions of Houghton MS Eng 530
rights.basis: Harvard's digitized-content terms state that it does not assert copyright over digital reproductions of public-domain works in the United States; this medieval manuscript is public domain there.
notes: Users remain responsible for rights in other jurisdictions and for applicable privacy, cultural, and contractual restrictions.

rights.component: Huntington digital reproductions of public-domain HM 144
rights.basis: The Huntington does not require permission to quote or publish material in the public domain or for which it does not own copyright, while asking users to cite the specific item; the medieval manuscript is public domain.
notes: The researcher remains responsible for any third-party rights and should cite HM 144 and The Huntington.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Caius manuscript wording, capitalization, errors, and unpunctuated verse stream
editorial_practices.representation: Close transcription with Borgstrom's punctuation, normalized capitalization policy, and correction of a few mere transcription errors
editorial_practices.description: Borgstrom explicitly says the poem is printed in close accordance with the manuscript, that proper names receive capitals, other manuscript capitals are lowered, and punctuation is his own.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Present lines 321-364 physically supplied on manuscript pp. 481-482 after an accidental omission, with insertion directions on pp. 476 and 482
editorial_practices.representation: Lines restored after present line 320, with duplicated line and misplaced marginal lines reconciled from rhyme, context, and scribal directions
editorial_practices.description: This is a reading-order reconstruction within one witness, not collation from a second source or evidence for a second work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviation signs, including ambiguous -er/-yr and plural endings
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters printed in italics; Borgstrom discusses choosing conventional -er for an ambiguous sign
editorial_practices.description: CME preserves 206 italic HI elements from the print layer. Without a Caius facsimile, those italics support Borgstrom's editorial expansion typography but cannot independently establish the original letterforms.
editorial_practices.scope: Poem pp. 508-525 and linguistic discussion at printed pp. 505-507

editorial_practices.representation: Poem pp. 508-525 only; title and ten pages of manuscript, attribution, linguistic, and metrical study omitted
editorial_practices.description: Footnotes attached to the poem are retained, but the preceding modern study is not part of CME00089.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete and badly arranged Lambeth MS 306 text versus the fuller MS 853 text
editorial_practices.representation: MS 306 retained on even pages and MS 853 printed opposite on odd pages
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall says he deliberately preserved the poorer MS 306 text and arrangement as an instructive witness error while using MS 853 to prevent readers from mistaking it for the best text.

evidence.citation: Huntington Digital Library IIIF manifest for HM 144.
evidence.summary: Supports the exact item and catalog identifiers, late-fifteenth-/early-sixteenth-century date, English paper codex, 152-leaf extent, 319-canvas complete sequence, detailed contents including the Complaint at ff. 1-9v, and rights-policy link.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of Huntington HM 144 boundary images and complete IIIF inventory.
evidence.locator: Bundle images 000012-000029; first and final provider canvases
evidence.summary: Confirms Complaint mapping at ff. 1r-9v / canvases 12-29 and complete published coverage from front cover through spine.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that the Library does not require permission to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, assigns rights investigation to the researcher, and asks for specific item citation.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record for the EEBO reproduction of The remors of conscyence, STC 20881.3.
evidence.summary: Supports Lichfield attribution, circa-1510 Wynkyn de Worde imprint, Cambridge University Library original, imperfect extent after a6, EEBO reel 1360:11, and subscription-mediated digital access.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Political, Religious, and Love Poems, EETS o.s. 15 (London, 1866).
evidence.locator: Preface pp. xviii-xix; parallel Complaint texts printed pp. 160-203 / PDF pp. 220-263
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the editor, publication, complete volume, deliberate facing-page treatment, incomplete and badly arranged MS 306 copy, fuller MS 853 text, exact printed boundaries, and comparison relationship. Direct inspection corrects DIMEV v1.0's inconsistent Furnivall page citation of 198-232.

open_questions.question: Can Gonville and Caius College supply or authorize a complete color facsimile of MS 174/95, including the physical pp. 476 and 481-482 evidence for Borgstrom's reordered passage?
next_steps: Ask for existing complete photography or a whole-object quotation covering binding, endleaves, blanks, every page, and any separately imaged edges.
Request exact confirmation of the pp. 469-482 pagination and the insertion directions on pp. 476, 481, and 482.
Obtain written terms for private research, public Git redistribution, and commercial or noncommercial publication before sharing supplied images.

open_questions.question: How should DIMEV v1.0's thirteen witnesses be reconciled with Appleford and Watson's statement that the Complaint survives in fourteen manuscripts and two de Worde editions?
next_steps: Consult the complete 2011 article and its witness notes.
Audit later DIMEV revisions, NIMEV corrections, manuscript reidentifications, and all three reported de Worde imprints.
Keep newly identified witnesses collateral unless historical evidence connects them to Borgstrom or another modeled edition.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV v1.0 cite Furnivall 1866 pp. 198-232 for Lambeth MSS 306 and 853 when the inspected complete 1866 volume prints their parallel Complaint texts on pp. 160-203?
next_steps: Check whether DIMEV refers to a later reissue, supplementary pagination, or a simple inherited error.
Retain the directly inspected 1866 pagination at the edition layer and preserve DIMEV's citation as a source-data discrepancy.
Compare all listed Furnivall issues before asserting identical pagination.

open_questions.question: Where precisely on NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1 f. 170v does the Complaint end and DIMEV record 2940's Song of Love to Jesus begin?
next_steps: Transcribe f. 170v and the adjacent recto and compare both DIMEV incipits and explicits.
Consult Hardman's 2000 facsimile introduction and a current contents handlist.
Until resolved, retain f. 170v / canvas 356 as a shared boundary surface in the whole-codex work portion.

open_questions.question: What are the textual families, compositional history, and best critical text across the manuscript and early-print tradition?
next_steps: Collate the five cached complete witness facsimiles against Borgstrom's Caius text and Furnivall's Lambeth texts.
Acquire or consult the remaining complete witnesses through their recorded routes.
Distinguish substantive variants, scribal restructuring, early-print revision, attribution history, and editor-supplied readings before adding family edges.

review_notes: Only Caius MS 174/95 is upstream of Borgstrom and CME00089. The twelve other DIMEV witnesses and Furnivall's edition remain collateral supporting relationships.

Complete public facsimiles are cached for Trinity R.3.21, NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1, Lambeth MS 306, Houghton MS Eng 530, and Huntington HM 144. Every complete object retains adjacent works and binding matter, while work_portion records the narrower Complaint range.
No authentication barrier, CAPTCHA, login, provider extraction restriction, or unavailable BL image endpoint was bypassed. Request and subscription routes are recorded instead.
The direct Caius facsimile remains the most important missing source because it is needed to check Borgstrom's italics, line displacement, marginal supplies, and exact page cross-references.
